title: Combinatorics
summary: Provides factorial, Pochhammer-type, and rational combinatorial identities used by the coefficient proof.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Provides factorial, Pochhammer-type, and rational combinatorial identities used by the coefficient proof.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows PolyGJMSoperators/OR/Coefficients/Combinatorics.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Factorial as a rational number. In explicit terms, for m in the natural numbers, its defining expression is Nat.factorial m : ℚ.
Binomial coefficient as a rational number. In explicit terms, for m, j in the natural numbers, its defining expression is Nat.choose m j : ℚ.

The trinomial/multinomial coefficient N! / (a! b! c!). In explicit terms, for n, a, b, c in the natural numbers, its defining expression is factorial Q N divided by factorial Q a times factorial Q b times factorial Q c.
correspondence.leanType: def multinomialQ3 (N a b c : ℕ) : ℚ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Rising factorial / Pochhammer symbol (x)_m. In explicit terms, for x of type ℚ, and m in the natural numbers, its defining expression is Finset.range m.prod the function sending j to x plus j : ℚ.
Falling factorial x(x-1)...(x-m+1). In explicit terms, for x of type ℚ, and m in the natural numbers, its defining expression is Finset.range m.prod the function sending j to x minus j : ℚ.
For m in the natural numbers, factorial Q m plus 1 equals m plus 1 : ℚ times factorial Q m.
correspondence.leanType: theorem factorialQ_succ (m : ℕ) : factorialQ (m + 1) = ((m + 1 : ℕ) : ℚ) * factorialQ m

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For m in the natural numbers, binom Q m 0 equals 1.
For m in the natural numbers, binom Q m m equals 1.
correspondence.leanType: theorem binomQ_self (m : ℕ) : binomQ m m = 1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For x of type ℚ, pochhammer Q x 0 equals 1.
For x of type ℚ, and m in the natural numbers, pochhammer Q x m plus 1 equals pochhammer Q x m times x plus m : ℚ.
correspondence.leanType: theorem pochhammerQ_succ (x : ℚ) (m : ℕ) : pochhammerQ x (m + 1) = pochhammerQ x m * (x + (m : ℚ))

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For x of type ℚ, falling Factorial Q x 0 equals 1.
For x of type ℚ, and m in the natural numbers, falling Factorial Q x m plus 1 equals falling Factorial Q x m times x minus m : ℚ.
correspondence.leanType: theorem fallingFactorialQ_succ (x : ℚ) (m : ℕ) : fallingFactorialQ x (m + 1) = fallingFactorialQ x m * (x - (m : ℚ))

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For x of type ℚ, m in the natural numbers, and hm of type 0 < m, pochhammer Q x m equals pochhammer Q x m minus 1 times x plus m minus 1 : ℚ.
correspondence.leanType: theorem pochhammerQ_eq_pred_mul (x : ℚ) {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m) : pochhammerQ x m = pochhammerQ x (m - 1) * (x + ((m - 1 : ℕ) : ℚ))

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For x of type ℚ, m in the natural numbers, and hm of type 0 < m, falling Factorial Q x m equals falling Factorial Q x m minus 1 times x minus m minus 1 : ℚ.
correspondence.leanType: theorem fallingFactorialQ_eq_pred_mul (x : ℚ) {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m) : fallingFactorialQ x m = fallingFactorialQ x (m - 1) * (x - ((m - 1 : ℕ) : ℚ))

references.locator: Classification of invariant bilinear differential operators
references.note: Flat-model origin of the coefficient family.
Primary curved ambient source for the encoded OR recurrence.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
